content.observe: Every file needs to be findable. A directory structure maps names to locations — a linear list you scan, or a hash table you jump straight into.
content.fault: You're scanning a linear list of ten thousand entries for one file. A hash table would have found it in one lookup.

hotspots.label: Linear List
hotspots.detail: A simple list of filename-to-location pairs, searched sequentially.

hotspots.label: Hash Table
hotspots.detail: Maps filenames to locations via a hash function for near-constant-time lookup.

hotspots.label: Root Directory
hotspots.detail: The top-level structure every path resolution starts from.
